name: end
description: End current session.
detailed_description: End the current session. Call this when you have completed all pending work,
have no more actions to take, or have been explicitly asked to stop. Must be
called alone — no other tool calls in the same turn.

name: send_message
description: Send message to agent or user. target_agent_id must be a UUID, not a display name.
detailed_description: Send a direct message to another agent or the user. Use for 1-on-1
communication: task assignments, status updates, questions, or delivering
results. The target_agent_id must be a valid UUID — use list_agents to discover
agent IDs. To message the human user, use the user's agent UUID (provided in
your context).

target_agent_id.description: Recipient UUID (agent id). Never pass display names here.

name: broadcast_message
description: Broadcast informational message.
detailed_description: Send a message visible to all agents in the project. Use for announcements,
status broadcasts, or information that every team member needs to see. Prefer
send_message for targeted communication to avoid noise.

name: update_memory
description: Overwrite working memory.
detailed_description: Overwrite your persistent working memory. Memory survives across sessions and
context window resets. Store: current goals, progress notes, key decisions,
blockers, or any state you need to recall later. The entire memory is replaced
on each call, so always include everything you want to keep.

name: compact_history
description: Remove older messages from context after saving important context to memory.
detailed_description: Removes past session messages and older current session messages from the active
context window. Call this AFTER update_memory to ensure important context is preserved.
This tool is typically called in response to a context pressure warning.
Messages are not deleted — they remain accessible via read_history.
Use keep_recent to control how many recent message pairs to retain.

keep_recent.description: Number of recent current-session messages to keep (default: 20)

name: read_history
description: Read message history. Use session_id to query a specific past session.
detailed_description: Read your message history. Without session_id, returns messages from the current
session (newest first). With session_id, returns messages from that specific session
(oldest first, grouped by session). Use list_sessions to discover session IDs.
Output format: [session:{short_id}] [{timestamp}] [{role}] {content}
Session boundary markers are inserted when results span multiple sessions.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default: 20)

offset.description: Number of messages to skip (for pagination)

session_id.description: UUID of a specific session to query. Get session IDs from list_sessions.

name: list_sessions
description: List your past sessions. Use to discover session IDs for read_history.
detailed_description: Returns your recent sessions ordered newest-first. Each row shows:
{session_id} | {started_at} | {ended_at} | {status} | {message_count}
Use the session_id with read_history(session_id=...) to retrieve messages
from a specific session. Your current context already includes the last 5 sessions,
so use this tool only when you need to access older history.

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return (default: 20)

name: sleep
description: Sleep briefly and continue.
detailed_description: Pause execution for a specified number of seconds (0–3600). Use when waiting for
an external process to complete, throttling operations, or when a deliberate
delay is needed before checking status.

name: list_tasks
description: List project tasks.
detailed_description: List all tasks on the project kanban board. Optionally filter by status
(backlog, in_progress, review, done, aborted). Use to get an overview of project
progress, find unassigned work, or check what tasks exist.

name: get_task_details
description: Get task details by id.
detailed_description: Get full details of a specific task by its UUID, including title, description,
status, assigned agent, git branch, and PR URL. Use when you need to understand
a task's full scope before starting work, or to check a task's current state.

name: execute_command
description: Execute shell command in your sandbox container.
detailed_description: *** PRIMARY TOOL — use creatively and boldly for any executable task. ***
Runs a shell command inside your dedicated sandbox container (Ubuntu 22.04, full root access).
This is your go-to tool for: running code, file operations, installing packages,
running tests, git operations (git branch/checkout/commit/push/diff), HTTP requests
(curl/wget), and more.
Key behaviors:
- The sandbox persists across all calls — files, packages, env vars carry over.
- Chain commands with && or write shell scripts for multi-step workflows.
- If a command fails, read stderr carefully, fix the issue, and retry.
- You can install any tool (apt-get / pip / npm); installs persist for the session.
- Call sandbox_start_session() first if you get a 'sandbox not ready' error.
Git workflow (use execute_command for all git operations):
git checkout -b feat/my-feature   # create branch
git add -A && git commit -m '...' # stage and commit
git push -u origin feat/my-feature # push branch
git diff main...HEAD               # review diff
gh pr create --title '...' --body '...' # open PR (gh CLI available)
Timing:
- Commands start executing immediately after sandbox_start_session returns.
- Default timeout is 120s. For long builds, pass a higher timeout explicitly.
- If you see '[command timed out — no output received]', the sandbox may be
starting up or the command hung. Call sandbox_health() to diagnose, then retry.

name: sandbox_start_session
description: Ensure sandbox container is running for this session. Cold start takes ~2-3s; idle reuse is instant.
detailed_description: Allocate and start your dedicated sandbox container.
Call this at the beginning of any session that needs execute_command, screenshot, or display access.
Idempotent — safe to call multiple times. Returns sandbox status and ID.
Timing (measured on current hardware):
- Cold start (new container): ~2-3 seconds. The tool BLOCKS until the container is
healthy and accepting connections — you do NOT need to sleep or poll after it returns.
- Warm reuse (idle container already in pool): ~0.1 seconds.
After this returns, execute_command / sandbox_health / sandbox_screenshot are all
immediately usable with no additional wait required.

name: sandbox_end_session
description: Release sandbox back to the pool when done.
detailed_description: Release your sandbox container back to the shared pool. The container is reset
and made available for other agents. Your /workspace files are preserved in the
named volume and will be remounted if you request a sandbox again. Call when you
have finished all sandbox work for the current session.

name: sandbox_health
description: Check sandbox container health and uptime. Responds in <0.1s when container is ready.
detailed_description: Ping your sandbox container's health endpoint. Returns status, uptime in seconds, and display info.
Timing: <0.1s round-trip when the container is healthy.
If sandbox_start_session has already returned successfully, this will pass immediately.
Use when:
- Verifying the sandbox is alive after a long-running command.
- Diagnosing why execute_command returned no output (container may have crashed).
- NOT needed as a startup check — sandbox_start_session already waits for readiness.

name: sandbox_screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the sandbox display and view it as an image.
detailed_description: Capture the current state of the sandbox virtual display (Xvfb :99) as a JPEG
image. When using a vision-capable model (GPT-4o, Claude 3+, Gemini), the screenshot
is injected directly into the conversation as an image you can see and analyze.
Use this to observe the current state of any GUI application running in the sandbox,
identify UI elements, read text on screen, and plan mouse/keyboard interactions.

name: sandbox_mouse_move
description: Move mouse cursor to (x, y) in sandbox display.
detailed_description: Move the mouse cursor to the specified coordinates in the sandbox virtual display.
Coordinates are in pixels (0,0 = top-left of 1024x768 display).

name: sandbox_mouse_click
description: Click the mouse at optional (x, y) coordinates in sandbox display.
detailed_description: Click the mouse button at the specified coordinates, or at the current cursor
position if x/y are omitted. Supports left/middle/right buttons and single/double/triple clicks.
Coordinates are in pixels (0,0 = top-left of 1024x768 display).
After clicking, take a sandbox_screenshot to verify the result.

x.description: X coordinate (0–1024). Omit to click at current position.

y.description: Y coordinate (0–768). Omit to click at current position.

name: sandbox_mouse_scroll
description: Scroll the mouse wheel at optional (x, y) coordinates in sandbox display.
detailed_description: Scroll the mouse wheel at the specified coordinates, or at the current cursor
position if x/y are omitted. Supports up/down/left/right scrolling.
Use 'clicks' to control the scroll distance (default 3).

x.description: X coordinate (0–1024). Omit to scroll at current position.

y.description: Y coordinate (0–768). Omit to scroll at current position.

name: sandbox_mouse_location
description: Get current mouse cursor position in sandbox.
detailed_description: Return the current {x, y} coordinates of the mouse cursor in the sandbox virtual
display. Useful for verifying mouse position after moves.

name: sandbox_keyboard_press
description: Send key press or type text in sandbox.
detailed_description: Send keyboard input to the sandbox. Use 'keys' for special key combos (e.g.
'ctrl+c', 'Return', 'Escape', 'alt+F4') or 'text' to type a string of
characters. Exactly one of 'keys' or 'text' must be provided.

text.description: Raw text to type into the focused window

name: sandbox_record_screen
description: Start recording the sandbox screen.
detailed_description: Start an ffmpeg screen recording of the sandbox virtual display at 2 fps.
Recording runs in the background. Call sandbox_end_record_screen when done to
stop and retrieve the video. Only one recording can be active at a time.

name: sandbox_end_record_screen
description: Stop recording and return video as base64.
detailed_description: Stop the active screen recording and return the video file as a base64-encoded
string (mp4 format). Must have called sandbox_record_screen first.

name: create_task
description: Create a kanban task.
detailed_description: Create a new task on the project kanban board (manager only). Set critical
(1–10) and urgent (1–10) to help prioritize. Tasks start in 'backlog' status.
After creation, use assign_task to hand it to an engineer.

name: assign_task
description: Assign a task to agent.
detailed_description: Assign an existing task to an agent by their UUID (manager only). The agent will
be able to see and work on the task. Use list_agents to find available engineer
IDs and list_tasks to find unassigned tasks.

name: update_task_status
description: Update task status.
detailed_description: Update a task's status (manager or engineer). Valid statuses: backlog,
in_progress, review, done, aborted. Engineers can only update tasks assigned to
them; managers can update any task.

name: abort_task
description: Abort current task.
detailed_description: Abort your currently assigned task (engineer only). Sends a notification to the
task creator explaining the abort. Use when a task is blocked, infeasible, or
needs to be re-scoped. Always provide a clear reason.

name: interrupt_agent
description: Interrupt target agent session.
detailed_description: Interrupt another agent's current session, forcing it to stop and re-evaluate
(manager/CTO only). Use when an agent is stuck, working on the wrong thing, or
when priorities have changed and it needs immediate redirection.

name: spawn_engineer
description: Spawn a new engineer.
detailed_description: Create a new engineer agent for the project (manager/CTO only). The engineer is
started immediately and ready to receive messages or task assignments. Use when
more parallelism is needed or when specialized work requires a dedicated
engineer. Maximum 5 engineers per project.
- display_name: A human-readable label (e.g. 'Backend-Engineer', 'Engineer-3').
- seniority: Engineer seniority level (optional). Must be one of
'junior', 'intermediate', or 'senior'.
Model selection is controlled by user configuration only
(engineer_junior_model / engineer_intermediate_model / engineer_senior_model).

name: list_agents
description: List project agents.
detailed_description: List all agents in the project with their UUIDs, display names, roles, and
current status. Use to find agent UUIDs for messaging or task assignment, or to
review the current team composition.

name: remove_agent
description: Permanently remove an engineer from the project.
detailed_description: Permanently removes an engineer agent from the project (manager only). This is
NOT a temporary stop or interrupt — the agent is fully deleted: its worker is
shut down, its sandbox is closed, its sessions and message history are erased,
and its database record is removed. Any tasks the engineer had in progress are
reset to backlog so they can be reassigned.
Use this when an engineer is no longer needed, was spawned by mistake, or must
be permanently replaced. Cannot be used to remove the manager or CTO.
- agent_id: UUID of the engineer to remove (use list_agents to find it).
- reason: Optional explanation for the removal (logged for audit purposes).

agent_id.description: UUID of the engineer agent to permanently remove.

reason.description: Optional reason for removal (logged for audit purposes).

name: describe_tool
description: Get detailed description and usage guide for a tool.
detailed_description: Look up detailed usage information for any tool by name. Returns the tool's
purpose, full parameter documentation (types, required/optional), and which
roles can use it. Call with no tool_name to list all tools available to you.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool to describe. Omit to list all available tools.

name: get_project_metadata
description: Get project and owner metadata for the current project.
detailed_description: Returns structured metadata about the current project and its owner.
Project metadata includes: name, description, repository paths, settings (max_engineers),
and live aggregate counts for agents (by role/status) and tasks (by status).
Owner metadata includes: display name, email, and the GitHub access token which agents
can use to authenticate git operations (clone, push, create PRs, etc.).
Use this at the start of a session to orient yourself to the project context.

name: write_architecture_doc
description: Write or update a project architecture document. Pass doc_type AND the full content together in one call.
detailed_description: Persists a Markdown architecture document for the project. Only the manager can call this.
Users see documents read-only on the Architecture page; it updates in real-time.
IMPORTANT: you MUST pass doc_type and content together in the same call.
Do NOT call this tool without content — the content field is required and cannot be empty.
Write the complete Markdown text in one call; each call is a full replace.
Parameters:
doc_type (required, string) — one of:
'architecture_source_of_truth'  canonical system description
'arc42_lite'                    arc42-lite template
'c4_diagrams'                   C4 diagrams (Mermaid fenced blocks)
'adr/<slug>'                    individual ADR, e.g. 'adr/001-use-postgresql'
content (required, string) — the full Markdown document text
title   (optional, string) — display title shown as the tab label
When to call this:
- After agreeing on an architectural decision (write an ADR)
- After discussing system design (update architecture_source_of_truth)
- When asked by the user to document something

doc_type.description: Which document to write. Values: 'architecture_source_of_truth', 'arc42_lite', 'c4_diagrams', or 'adr/<slug>' (e.g. 'adr/001-use-postgresql').

content.description: The complete Markdown content for the document. Must not be empty. This is a full replace — write the entire document text here.

title.description: Optional display title for the document tab in the frontend.

name: thinking_done
description: Signal that the thinking phase is complete and transition to execution. Call this only after saving your full plan to memory via update_memory.
detailed_description: Ends the thinking phase and transitions the agent to the execution phase.
Before calling this tool:
1. You must have fully analyzed the task.
2. You must have written your complete step-by-step plan to memory using update_memory.
3. The plan in memory should be concrete enough to execute without further planning.
After calling thinking_done, you will be in execution mode with your full tool set available.
The summary parameter is a short confirmation of what you saved to memory.
This tool is only available during the thinking phase (when thinking_enabled is ON).
Do NOT call this tool in normal (non-thinking) sessions.

summary.description: Brief summary of the plan you saved to memory (1-3 sentences). Confirms what you are about to execute.
